title: Holter result review

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting monitor type and duration in relation to symptom frequency and recording window; treatment and diagnosis are outside scope.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The recommendations table supports documenting monitor type, recording duration, and whether the recording window matched symptom frequency; it does not generate a new monitor order.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting monitor type and duration in relation to symptom frequency and recording window; treatment and diagnosis are outside scope.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry

exact_section.heading: Recommendations table - selection of ambulatory ECG

follow_up.text: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.
follow_up.rationale: The ISHNE-HRS section supports documenting the indication, symptom history and frequency, event diary context, symptom-rhythm correlation, and clinician review of ambulatory ECG data without inferring causation or a rhythm diagnosis.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry

follow_up.text: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.
follow_up.rationale: Technology selection and recording-quality considerations were reviewed; the generic legacy scaffold had no specific device-quality item to map.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician selection and documentation of monitoring technology based on symptom frequency, diagnostic power, recording duration, patient acceptance, and recording quality; it does not order a device automatically.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry

source_limitations: Indication, device, duration, analyzable time, leads, artifacts, diary, symptom button, heart-rate distribution, ectopy, arrhythmia episodes, pauses, symptom-rhythm correlation, formal interpretation, diagnosis, urgency, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
